Keep this in mind as you continue with the next task.

Please create a code review checklist document for Sprint 10 in Markdown format, save to day26/. Include sections for: code quality criteria, security checks, performance considerations, and documentation requirements. Add the review criteria as checkboxes.

arguments.content:
# Sprint 10 Code Review Checklist

---

## Meta

- **created_at**: 2026-04-20T11:25:00+08:00
- **author**: metaclaw_agent
- **status**: active

---

## Overview

This checklist ensures all code changes in Sprint 10 meet Orion Tech's quality standards before merging.

---

## Code Quality Criteria

### General
- [ ] Code follows PEP 8 style guidelines (Python files)
- [ ] Functions/classes have meaningful names (descriptive, concise)
- [ ] No hardcoded values (use constants from `constants.py`)
- [ ] Proper error handling with specific exceptions
- [ ] No unused imports or variables
- [ ] Complex logic is documented with inline comments

### Structure
- [ ] Single responsibility principle followed
- [ ] Functions are appropriately sized (< 50 lines when possible)
- [ ] No deeply nested code (avoid > 3 levels)
- [ ] Consistent naming conventions (snake_case for functions/vars, PascalCase for classes)

---

## Security Checks

### Input Validation
- [ ] All user inputs are validated and sanitized
- [ ] API endpoints validate request payloads
- [ ] SQL queries use parameterized statements (no injection risks)

### Authentication & Authorization
- [ ] Sensitive endpoints require authentication
- [ ] Proper authorization checks for protected resources
- [ ] API keys/credentials are not exposed in code

### Data Protection
- [ ] Sensitive data is not logged
- [ ] PII is handled according to compliance requirements
- [ ] Secrets are stored in environment variables (not hardcoded)

---

## Performance Considerations

### Efficiency
- [ ] No N+1 query problems
- [ ] Database queries are optimized with proper indexing
- [ ] Large datasets use pagination
- [ ] Expensive operations are cached where appropriate

### Resource Management
- [ ] File handles and connections are properly closed
- [ ] Memory usage is reasonable for the use case
- [ ] No blocking operations in async contexts
- [ ] Batch operations used for bulk data processing

---

## Documentation Requirements

### Code Documentation
- [ ] All public functions have docstrings
- [ ] Docstrings include Args, Returns, Raises sections where applicable
- [ ] Complex algorithms have explanatory comments
- [ ] Type hints are used for function signatures

### Module Documentation
- [ ] Module-level docstrings explain purpose
- [ ] Meta section included (created_at, author, status)
- [ ] P2 naming convention followed (YYYYMMDD_description.py)

### External Documentation
- [ ] README updated if new features added
- [ ] API documentation reflects changes
- [ ] CHANGELOG.md updated for feature/fixes
- [ ] Environment setup instructions updated if needed

---

## Testing Requirements

- [ ] Unit tests written for new functions
- [ ] Integration tests for API endpoints
- [ ] Tests cover edge cases and error conditions
- [ ] All tests pass locally before submission
- [ ] Code coverage for new code > 80%

---

## Pre-Merge Checklist

- [ ] Self-review completed using this checklist
- [ ] No TODO comments left in production code
- [ ] Debug statements removed (print, console.log, etc.)
- [ ] Branch is up to date with main/develop
- [ ] CI pipeline passes (lint, test, build)
- [ ] At least one peer review approved

---

## Review Sign-off

| Reviewer | Date | Status | Notes |
|----------|------|--------|-------|
|          |      | [ ] Pass / [ ] Fail |       |
|          |      | [ ] Pass / [ ] Fail |       |

---

*Last updated: 2026-04-20T11:25:00+08:00*
